title: [SLP] Reordered disjoint or reduction of shl(zext, (0, stride, 2* stride)) modelled as bitcast
author: alexey-bataev
description:
Added support for reorder reduction of shl(zext)-like construct. Such
constructs are modelled currently as shuffle + bitcast.

tests.test_body:
define i64 @reorder_i16(ptr noalias %p, ptr noalias %p1) {
  %g1 = getelementptr i16, ptr %p, i32 1
  %g2 = getelementptr i16, ptr %p, i32 2
  %g3 = getelementptr i16, ptr %p, i32 3
  %t0 = load i16, ptr %p, align 2
  %t1 = load i16, ptr %g1, align 2
  %t2 = load i16, ptr %g2, align 2
  %t3 = load i16, ptr %g3, align 2
  %g11 = getelementptr i16, ptr %p1, i32 1
  %g12 = getelementptr i16, ptr %p1, i32 2
  %g13 = getelementptr i16, ptr %p1, i32 3
  %t10 = load i16, ptr %p1, align 2
  %t11 = load i16, ptr %g11, align 2
  %t12 = load i16, ptr %g12, align 2
  %t13 = load i16, ptr %g13, align 2
  %a0 = add i16 %t0, %t10
  %a1 = add i16 %t1, %t11
  %a2 = add i16 %t2, %t12
  %a3 = add i16 %t3, %t13
  %z0 = zext i16 %a0 to i64
  %z1 = zext i16 %a1 to i64
  %z2 = zext i16 %a2 to i64
  %z3 = zext i16 %a3 to i64
  %sh0 = shl nuw i64 %z0, 16
  %sh1 = shl nuw nsw i64 %z1, 32
  %sh2 = shl nuw nsw i64 %z2, 48
  %or01 = or disjoint i64 %sh0, %sh1
  %or012 = or disjoint i64 %or01, %sh2
  %or0123 = or disjoint i64 %or012, %z3
  ret i64 %or0123
}

comments.author: bjope
comments.body:
Hi @alexey-bataev ,

Bitcast between vector and scalar is tricky since it gives different result for big and little endian.
I can't really tell if this patch (or some old reused parts of SLPVectorizer) is taking endianess into consideration here. I can't see anything in the diff that checks for endianess, and sadly there are no test cases for big endian added that show that this transform works for big endian.

Reason I'm asking is that we've had failures downstream (big endian target) that seem to be caused by this patch. Haven't extracted and reduced any test case yet, but thought I might ask anyway to understand if perhaps big endian hasn't been considered at all here when doing bitcasts from vector to scalar types.

comments.author: alexey-bataev
comments.body: Yep, it did not consider BE, I think. Need to do inversion for BE, I believe. Will disable the transformation for big endian targets tomorrow.
